title: Gir Kybo Ren-Cha
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|Kybo Ren, a [[Pirate/Legends|space pirate]]|[[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]], the [[First Order]] [[Supreme Leader]], or [[Flynn Kybo]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]|linebreak=1}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:KyboRen-TPoT.png]] |name=Gir Kybo Ren-Cha |homeworld=[[Tarnoonga/Legends|Tarnoonga]]{{DB|character|kyboren|Ren-Cha, Gir Kybo}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Ropagu]]{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]]{{DroidsCite|The Pirates of Tarnoonga}} |feathers= |eyes=Dark brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Pirates of Tarnoonga]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|No one escapes the wrath of Kybo Ren!|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha|}} '''Gir Kybo Ren-Cha''', commonly known as '''Kybo Ren''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ropagu]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] active during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Operating out of a [[Kybo Ren's base|base]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tarnoonga/Legends|Tarnoonga]], Ren's [[Pirates of Tarnoonga|crew]] frequently raided [[Starship/Legends|shipping]] in the vicinity of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tammuz-an/Legends|Tammuz-an]]. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Ren's operation captured the [[Gladiator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Gladiator''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Demolisher]]'' from an Imperial repair dock to use in a raid on Tammuz-an itself. In order to weaken the [[Tammuz-an space navy]], Ren captured a [[King's Ransom (transport)|transport]] carrying [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] to the planet. However, the ship's crew escaped, forcing Ren to launch his [[Battle of Tammuz-an|attack]] early. As the ''Demolisher'' battled the [[Navy/Legends|naval forces]] of the Tammuz-an [[King/Legends|King]] [[Mon Julpa]], the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] sabotaged the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], leaving it disabled and leading to Ren's arrest. He was [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] on Tammuz-an but was subsequently rescued by his [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]], [[Jyn Obah]]. He then took [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Gerin Toda]], daughter of the [[Tammuz-an (species)/Legends|Tammuz-an]] warrior-[[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Malameu Toda]], as a hostage. Ren fled to the [[Bog moon of Bogden]] and demanded that Toda release his crew and turn Mon Julpa over to him in exchange for the return of his daughter. Toda agreed, but hid himself and a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Tammuz-an [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] aboard Ren's modified [[attack ship]], the [[Dianoga (starship)|''Dianoga'']], while it carried the pirates to [[Bogden/Legends|Bogden]]. When Ren attempted to renege on his promise to release Gerin, Toda's [[Troop/Legends|troops]] emerged, and Ren was recaptured in the ensuing [[Rescue of Gerin Toda|skirmish]]. ==Biography== ===Early career=== {{Quote|Welcome aboard the ''Demolisher''. It's small, but it's all we could… borrow from a repair dock.|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha|}} [[File:TarnoongaPirates-TPoT.png|thumb|left|200px|Kybo Ren leads a boarding party.]] A native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tarnoonga/Legends|Tarnoonga]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' the [[Ropagu]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Gir Kybo Ren-Cha, commonly known as Kybo Ren, was active as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. His mostly [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] [[Pirates of Tarnoonga|crew]] operated out of a [[Kybo Ren's base|base]] built into an island [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] extending from the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Ren's crew operated a number of captured Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], decorated with the symbol of his gang—a red stylized [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] [[Bone/Legends|skull]]—on their [[Solar collector/Legends|solar panel]] wings. Ren himself flew a modified [[attack ship]], the [[Dianoga (starship)|''Dianoga'']], which was similarly marked with the pirates' emblem. Ren frequently operated in the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] around the planet [[Tammuz-an/Legends|Tammuz-an]], conducting raids against [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] en route to the planet. He recognized that Tammuz-an was a world of great wealth and, in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],"[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. began to plan a major assault against the planet itself. In order to provide him with an advantage over Tammuz-an's [[Tammuz-an space navy|naval forces]], he stole the [[Gladiator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Gladiator''-class Star Destroyer]]''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'' ''[[Demolisher]]'' from an Imperial repair dock and converted it to serve as his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]].{{CSWECite|I|182|''Demolisher''}} Knowing that the Empire would come looking for their missing [[Starship/Legends|ship]], he made plans to scuttle it on a remote planet once it had served its purpose, destroying any evidence that would incriminate him in the theft. Shortly before moving against Tammuz-an, Ren was aboard the ''Dianoga'' when a small [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by a young woman named [[Jessica Meade]] entered the [[Tammuz-an system/Legends|system]], transporting the [[Tammuz-an (species)/Legends|Tammuz-an]] heir apparent, [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Mon Julpa]], back to his homeworld. Ren's ship attacked the [[Transport/Legends|transport]] and managed to evade the returning fire to maneuver into the other vessel's path. As Ren closed on his target and prepared to disable its [[Engine/Legends|engines]], the two ships collided. The pirates recovered quickly, but Meade and her passengers had [[Time/Legends|time]] to abandon ship before Ren opened fire once more, destroying the freighter. Unbeknownst to the pirates, Julpa's rival, [[Ko Zatec-Cha]], had hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]{{DroidsCite|The New King}} [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88B]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' to capture the Prince. Determined to see his [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] unharmed, IG-88B's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] fired on the Dianoga, allowing Meade and Julpa to safely reach Tammuz-an. ===Battle for Tammuz-an=== {{Quote|I know there is not enough fuel on Tammuz-an for a single fighter. And now that I have your fuel, they are defenseless—ripe for an attack.''"
"''You'll find out differently. Just try it.''"
"''I intend to. I have a little surprise I've been saving just for the occasion. A guarantee of victory.|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha and Jann Tosh|}} [[File:RenAndMeade-TPoT.png|thumb|right|200px|Ren captures Jessica Meade.]] A short time later, Ren learned of a [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] shipment being delivered to Tammuz-an for use by the planet's starfighter defenses. Knowing that without the fuel supply, Tammuz-an would be virtually defenseless and open to attack, Ren resolved to capture it. The newly instated [[King/Legends|King]] Mon Julpa anticipated Ren's actions and sent a decoy shipment ahead in an attempt to draw the pirate off. Ren's [[Spy/Legends|spies]], however, alerted the pirate captain to the decoy, and he allowed it to pass while waiting for the true shipment. The fuel transport [[King's Ransom (transport)|''King's Ransom'']] soon arrived, piloted by Jessica Meade and guarded by an [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]]''[[C-3PO: Tales of the Golden Droid]]'' flown by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] pilot [[Jann Tosh]]. Ren's TIE fighters attacked, drawing Tosh into a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]] and allowing Ren to reach the ''King's Ransom'' in the ''Dianoga''. Docking with the freighter, the pirates cut a hole through the other vessel's [[Hull/Legends|hull]], and Ren personally led the boarding party, claiming the ship, its crew, and cargo. When Tosh's [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], contacted his master to warn him, Ren ordered Tosh to surrender. The pilot agreed and the ''Dianoga'', still attached to Meade's ship, locked a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] onto Tosh's fighter before returning to Tarnoonga. Back at his base, Ren had the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] brought before him in his throne room. The pirate captain had become enamored with Meade's fiery spirit and held her captive as his "personal guest." With his crew making final preparations to attack Tammuz-an, Ren was unwilling to risk Tosh interfering with his plans and ordered the young pilot's [[Death/Legends|execution]]. Tosh, along with C-3PO and the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], were taken out on a [[Bantha-II cargo skiff/Legends|skiff]] over the nearby sea. Making Tosh and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] walk the plank, Ren left them to be devoured by the predatory [[miridon]], which inhabited the area, and returned to his base. Ren caught Meade attempting to escape from the quarters where she was being held. The pirate attempted to persuade her to stay with him voluntarily, promising to buy her kingdoms with the wealth of Tammuz-an. Meade pretended to be swayed by Ren's offer and, activating a nearby training [[Remote/Legends|remote]], asked him to demonstrate his skills. Ren duly obliged and chased the remote around the room in an attempt to catch it. With Ren momentarily distracted, Meade attempted to slip out of the room, only to be stopped by Ren's [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]],{{DroidsCite|The Revenge of Kybo Ren}} [[Jyn Obah]], who arrived to inform his captain that Tosh and the droids had survived and returned to the base for Meade. Leaving Obah to watch over Meade, Ren set off to catch the intruders. [[File:KyboRenBlaster-TPoT.png|thumb|left|200px|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha]] Ren soon arrived in the base's combined [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and sea dock to find it under attack from the miridon, which had followed Tosh into the base. Spotting Tosh and the droids, Ren attempted to apprehend them, but Meade, who had escaped from Obah, took him unawares and caught him in a [[Net/Legends|net]]. The pirate soon freed himself and ordered his crew to stop Tosh, Meade, and the droids before they escaped. However, his crew was busy trying to fend off the miridon, and the group managed to escape in the ''King's Ransom'' and Tosh's R-22. With his plans in jeopardy, Ren ordered the [[Battle of Tammuz-an|attack]] on Tammuz-an to begin immediately. As Ren's fighters launched, the pirate captain headed straight to the ''Demolisher'' and intercepted Tosh as the young pilot attempted to leave the [[Tarnoonga system/Legends|system]]. Tosh's R-22 was caught in a tractor beam and brought aboard the ''Demolisher'', where Ren had Tosh [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] and put C-3PO and R2-D2 to work loading [[Missile/Legends|torpedoes]]. With Tosh and the droids in custody, Ren made the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump to Tammuz-an in pursuit of Meade. The [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] reverted to realspace close to Meade's fuel transport, and Ren opened fire, destroying the other ship moments after Meade fled in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. The small explosion took Ren by surprise, and the pirate realized that the transport had been empty. At that moment, Tosh, who had been released by C-3PO and R2-D2 after they escaped during the trip to Tammuz-an, arrived on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. Tosh explained that the transport had been a decoy and the real fuel shipment had already reached Tammuz-an. Ren was furious at being deceived but remained confident that the ''Demolisher'' would ensure his victory over the approaching Tammuz-an [[Navy/Legends|navy]]. While his TIE fighters engaged |Tammuz-an R-22s, Ren prepared to fire at Mon Julpa's [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. However, unbeknownst to Ren, R2-D2 had sabotaged the ''Demolisher''{{'s}} torpedoes, loading them in reverse and causing them to backfire as they were launched. The resulting explosions crippled the Star Destroyer's engines and systems, leaving it disabled. ===Ren's revenge=== {{Quote|Tammuz-an has not seen the last of Kybo Ren.|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha|}} [[File:KyboRenEscapes-TRoKR.png|thumb|right|200px|Ren flees Tammuz-an with the captive Gerin Toda.]] Julpa dispatched transports to pick up the stranded pirates, and Ren was imprisoned in the dungeon of Julpa's [[Royal castle|castle]] in Tammuz-an's [[Tammuz-an capital city|capital city]]. Hoping to unite his people against any future invaders, Julpa began negotiating a peace treaty with the Tammuz-an warrior-[[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Malameu Toda]]. During the deliberations, Jyn Obah broke into the fortress and freed Ren from his cell. The two pirates made their way to the castle's landing pad, where they found Mon Julpa with Toda's daughter, Princess [[Gerin Toda|Gerin]]. Obah used a [[Stunner/Legends|stunner]] to incapacitate Julpa before Gerin got hold of the weapon and turned it on Ren. As C-3PO rushed to help, the pirate captain grabbed the droid and threw him into Gerin, causing her to drop the weapon. After using the stunner on Gerin, Ren boarded an R-22 Spearhead with her as his hostage and escaped, leaving Obah to be captured. Ren fled to the [[Bog moon of Bogden|bog moon]] of [[Bogden/Legends|Bogden]] where he established a makeshift prison in a [[Unidentified freighter (Bogden)|freighter]] hidden in the [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]], warning Gerin that if she tried to escape her cell she would be at the mercy of the wild [[sungwa]]s that infested the ship. The pirate contacted Toda and Julpa and demanded the release of his crew in exchange for the princess's safe return. The two Tammuz-an agreed but, having learned the location of Ren's hideout from Obah, dispatched Tosh, Meade, C-3PO and R2-D2 to mount a [[Rescue of Gerin Toda|rescue]]. After reaching the princess, the group attempted to escape the ship, only to come under attack from a pack of sungwas. Observing events from the freighter's bridge, Ren was confident that the interlopers would not escape the predators and contacted Toda and Julpa once more to inform them that the rescue had failed and to demand that Toda now turn Julpa over to him as a result of their deceit. Blaming Julpa for his daughter's kidnapping, Toda agreed. Meade and R2-D2, having split off from the rest of their group and evaded the sungwas, attempted to confront Ren, but were incapacitated by a [[Force field/Legends|force field]] as they reached the bridge. After contacting Obah aboard the ''Dianoga'' to ensure that his crew was not being followed, Ren left Meade and R2-D2 tied up in vines and set off to find Tosh, Gerin, and C-3PO. After Ren left, however, his prisoners freed themselves and used the ship's [[Intercom/Legends|intercom]] to warn their friends, who took cover as Ren searched the corridor in which they were hiding. Hearing a noise around the corner, Ren thought he had found his quarry but instead found himself facing a pack of sungwas. The predators gave chase, but Ren managed to evade them and proceeded to the hangar, where the ''Dianoga'' had arrived in time for Ren's crew to stop the rescue party as they attempted to escape. The captive Julpa told Ren to uphold his end of the bargain and release Gerin. When Ren refused, Julpa gave a signal and Toda emerged from a hidden compartment built into the hull of the ''Dianoga'', along with several guards. Ren attempted to escape during the ensuing [[Lightfight|firefight]] between his crew and the Tammuz-an [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], but he was intercepted by C-3PO and recaptured. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You're not leaving. Why, what about my offer?''"
"''I'd rather squeeze into a space suit with a mynock than stay here with you.''"
"''Ha! You've got the spirit, the fire I've been searching galaxies to find. With the spoils from Tammuz-an, I will buy you kingdoms.|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha and Jessica Meade|}} [[File:KyboRenThrone-TPoT.png|thumb|left|200px|Ren sits on his throne.]] Ren-Cha was a short, overweight Ropagu with dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] who could be easily recognized by his long, dangling mustache and small goatee. Ren typically wore a [[Armor/Legends|helmet]], under which little of his black [[Hair/Legends|hair]] remained by the early years of the Galactic Empire. An infamous pirate''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' who was willing to kill to achieve his goals, Ren commanded a large, well-organized pirate group. Although he usually preyed on convoys traveling around Tammuz-an, Ren was known to launch more audacious raids from time to time. He stole Imperial military equipment, including the ''Gladiator''-class Star Destroyer ''Demolisher'' and numerous TIE fighters, and ultimately planned to attack Tammuz-an itself to steal the planet's wealth. Ren's success made him extremely egotistical; he sat upon a throne in his base on Tarnoonga, frequently bragged about his victories and referred to himself by name. Ren demanded instant obedience from those under him, barking out raspy-voiced commands to anyone within earshot, and frequently emitted a raucous, bellowing peal of laughter. Although Ren personally led his pirates on raids, he was careful to ensure his own safety. When Jyn Obah rescued him from custody on Tammuz-an, Ren fled and left Obah to be captured after the pair were confronted while escaping. However, Ren did later attempt to secure the release of his crew, including Obah, in exchange for the return of Princess Gerin. Ren became captivated by Jessica Meade's fiery spirit and intended to keep her captive on Tarnoonga as his "personal guest," promising to buy her kingdoms with the wealth of Tammuz-an. However, the pirate captain's advances were not reciprocated, and his attempts to impress Meade almost allowed her to escape. Ren's crew was mostly comprised of non-Humans, and he could speak a [[Pirates of Tarnoonga language|language]] used among his crew in addition to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. ==Behind the scenes== Gir Kybo Ren-Cha was created by [[Peter Sauder]] for the [[1985]] [[Nelvana]] animated series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']]. Ren made a brief appearance in the second episode of the second story arc, "[[The New King]]," which was first broadcast on [[October 12]], 1985.{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} He then served as the main antagonist in the two subsequent episodes, "[[The Pirates of Tarnoonga]]" and "[[The Revenge of Kybo Ren]]," broadcast on [[October 19]] and [[October 26]] respectively. In all of his appearances, Ren was voiced by [[Don Francks]].{{WebCite|url=https://www2.bfi.org.uk/films-tv-people/4ce2ba1530147|text=Don Francks|archivedate=20201021142344}} The character was only called Kybo Ren-Cha in ''Droids''; his full name was revealed in the [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded|second edition]] of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' in [[1994]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Gir Kybo Ren-Cha}} *{{DroidsCite|The New King}} {{1st}} *{{DroidsCite|The Pirates of Tarnoonga}} *''[[The Pirates of Tarnoonga: A Droid Adventure]]'' *{{DroidsCite|The Revenge of Kybo Ren}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{1stID|Gir Kybo Ren-Cha}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/89/indexp3.html|text=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues|int=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|monjulpa|Julpa, Mon}} *{{DB|character|jessicameade|Meade, Jessica}} *{{DB|character|jynobah|Obah, Jyn}} *{{DB|character|kyboren|Ren-Cha, Gir Kybo}} *{{DB|location|tammuzan|Tammuz-an}} *{{DB|character|janntosh|Tosh, Jann}} *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} *{{SW|url=news/from-boonta-to-baobab-droids-and-the-star-wars-prequels|text=From Boonta to Baobab: ''Droids'' and the ''Star Wars'' Prequels|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Gir Kybo Ren-Cha |es=Gir Kybo Ren-Cha |ka=გირ კაიბო რენ-ჩა |enFA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ren-Cha, Gir Kybo}} [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pirates]] [[Category:Ropagu]]
